{{Quote|Send me a Baktoid model E-5, fully operational and armed, to my quarters.|Raith Sienar, to [[Kett (Galactic Republic)|Kett]]|<ref name="Rogue Planet" />}}
The '''E-5 battle droid''' was a model of [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] manufactured by [[Baktoid Combat Automata/Legends|Baktoid Combat Automata]]. Based on the [[E4 baron droid]], it was an interim successor to the [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]] and was quickly made obsolete by the [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|B2 super battle droid]].

==Characteristics==
{{Quote|As I told you, these droids have been altered to be capable of independence, and they are considerably more rugged than earlier models.|[[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]], to [[Raith Sienar/Legends|Raith Sienar]]|<ref name="Rogue Planet" />}}
Tall and thin, the E-5 [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] was based on the design of the [[E4 baron droid]].<ref name="E-5 battle droid">{{CSWECite|I|224|E-5 battle droid}}</ref> Featuring [[Armor/Legends|armor]] heavier than that of the [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]], they were equipped with individual [[Intelligence matrix|intelligence matrices]],<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Droids">''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref> allowing them to operate independently of a [[Central Control Computer/Legends|Central Control Computer]]. However, their lack of intelligence was their greatest weakness,<ref name="Rogue Planet" /> and an E-5's limited [[Verbobrain/Legends|verbobrain]]<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Droids" /> was said to be as slow as that of any [[Droid tank/Legends|droid tank]].<ref name="Rogue Planet" />

==History==
After the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation's]] defeat at the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]]<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Droids" /> in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="The New Essential Chronology">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> the E-5 was secretly<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Droids" /> designed and manufactured by [[Baktoid Combat Automata/Legends|Baktoid Combat Automata]]<ref name="Rogue Planet" /> as an interim successor to the B1 battle [[Droid/Legends|droid]].<ref name="E-5 battle droid" /> As a temporary measure, they were produced in small numbers while the Trade Federation developed the [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|B2 super battle droid]],<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Droids" /> quickly rendering the E-5 obsolete.<ref name="E-5 battle droid" />

In the years prior to the [[Separatist Crisis]], a contingent of E-5 battle droids, alongside several B1s and [[Special Tactics trooper|Republic troopers]] would serve aboard the [[Captor-class heavy munitions cruiser]], ''[[Admiral Korvin]]'', in [[29 BBY/Legends|29 BBY]] as a part of the [[Outland Regions Security Force/Legends|Republic Outland Regions Security Force]] under the joint command of [[Raith Sienar/Legends|Raith Sinear]] and [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]]. These E-5 battle droids would be deployed by the Republic to the living planet [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]], which had began producing strange living starfighters after both it and a [[Vergere|single]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] were [[First Battle of Zonama Sekot|attacked]] by an [[Yuuzhan Vong|unknown entity]]. In response, Tarkin and Sinear's force would [[Second Battle of Zonama Sekot|attack]] the living planet, only for the planet retreat into the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] after their task force was encountered by Jedi Knight [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and his [[Padawan/Legends|padawan learner]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]].<ref name="Rogue Planet" />
